name: First Missouri
description_short: First Missouri Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in St. Louis, serving 7,966 members across 2 branches since 1927.
description_long:
First Missouri Credit Union was chartered on January 1, 1927, and is headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 95 years of history, it operates as a federally insured state-chartered credit union with $55.7 million in assets and 7,966 active members. The institution is a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank system and maintains NCUA deposit insurance protection for member funds.

First Missouri offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products. On the depository side, members can access share accounts, share certificates with low minimum balances, business share accounts, and no-cost share drafts. For borrowing needs, the credit union provides real estate loans, mortgage loans, business loans, consumer loans, share-secured credit cards, overdraft protection, and risk-based lending. Electronic banking services include mobile banking, internet home banking, ATM/debit card programs, check cashing, money orders, bill payment, remote deposit capture, and account aggregation.

The credit union distinguishes itself through community-focused membership eligibility. Membership is open to anyone living or working in St. Louis or Jefferson County, with eligibility also extended to immediate relatives of members and employees of certain local companies. The institution offers financial education and counseling services, bilingual support, and operates a no-surcharge ATM network. With leadership under CEO Steven Ogolin, the credit union combines traditional brick-and-mortar banking with modern digital services.

First Missouri is best suited for individuals and small businesses with ties to the St. Louis and Jefferson County areas seeking community-based banking with competitive rates and personalized service. However, as a smaller institution with only $55.7 million in assets and 2 physical branches, it lacks the extensive branch network, lending capacity, and technology infrastructure of larger regional or national banks. The geographic membership requirement may also limit accessibility for those outside the service area.
services: Share accounts and business share accounts
Share certificates with low minimum balance requirements
Real estate and indirect mortgage loans
Business loans and indirect business loans
Consumer loans and share-secured credit cards
Mobile banking and internet home banking
Remote deposit capture and check cashing
ATM/debit card programs and prepaid debit cards
No-cost bill payment and money orders
Account aggregation and external account transfers
Overdraft protection and overdraft lines of credit
pros: NCUA deposit insurance protection on all member accounts
Open membership policy for St. Louis and Jefferson County residents, plus relatives and local company employees
No-cost share drafts (checking accounts) and no-cost bill payer service
No-surcharge ATM network access for members
Financial education and counseling services available
Full suite of electronic banking: mobile banking, remote deposit capture, internet access, and account aggregation
95+ year history serving the St. Louis community since 1927
cons: Only 2 physical branch locations limits convenience for broader geographic service
Relatively small asset base ($55.7M) may limit lending capacity for larger loan requests
Limited to St. Louis and Jefferson County geographic service area, restricting membership eligibility
Not yet rated on the platform indicates limited public review feedback to evaluate service quality
Smaller institution may have less competitive rates and product offerings compared to major regional banks
best_for: St. Louis and Jefferson County residents seeking community-based credit union banking
Small business owners in the service area needing business lending and depository services
Individuals seeking financial counseling and education alongside banking services
Members wanting to avoid ATM fees through no-surcharge network access
diagnosis: First Missouri Credit Union is best for St. Louis and Jefferson County residents seeking traditional community-based banking with deposit insurance, financial counseling, and convenient digital services. The main caveat is its limited geographic service area and small asset base, which may restrict lending capacity and product competitiveness compared to larger regional institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
